texto: And as if he had read her thoughts, the old man murmured, 'What a blessing it is to die in your own bed, under your own roof, with your family surrounding you, full of the knowledge that you have lived as thoroughly as you wanted to.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Anita Rau Badami: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “And as if he had read her thoughts, the old man murmured, 'What a blessing it is to die in your o…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que sabedoria, belonging, dying pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dying.

frase_original: And as if he had read her thoughts, the old man murmured, 'What a blessing it is to die in your own bed, under your own roof, with your family surrounding you, full of the knowledge that you have lived as thoroughly as you wanted to.
